title: Aureline release-truth summary, ship-or-no-ship posture, and decision-right linkage contract
description: Boundary schema for one release_truth_summary_record frozen in docs/governance/decision_right_and_release_truth_contract.md. The record is the reusable projection that the governance dashboard, the milestone scorecard, the release-evidence shiproom packet, the support bundle, the public claim manifest, the compatibility-window report, and the enterprise / managed-tenant review quote when the live ship-or-no-ship posture for a release candidate, an LTS line, a workflow-bundle certification, or a public-truth publication has to be visible and exportable from the same object family that names the underlying decision-right cards. Every summary names a typed release target, a typed ship-or-no-ship posture (ship satisfied, ship with named narrowing, hold blockers open, hold waived blockers within expiry, no-ship blockers, no-ship decision-route unresolved, rollback or yank in flight), the typed open blockers (each pinned to a decision-right card), the typed waived blockers with expiry proximity, the count of stale evidence rows holding ship plus the linked stale-evidence refs, the typed mitigations (narrower scope, slower path, less portable, temporarily blocked, waiver-held, partial-profile, early-signal drift, escalated-pending-decision), the typed export-packet action (publish, hold, rollback, yank, repin, mirror-only, no-action, escalate-for-break-glass), the machine-readable decision-right card refs that gate the posture, and the typed degraded-state label that surfaces MUST render when ownership is unresolved or no decision route is named. A 'ship satisfied' posture cannot ride open blockers; a 'no-ship decision-route unresolved' summary cannot collapse into a release-packet 'go' chip; a waived blocker past its expiry cannot render as held. Personal handles, raw email addresses, raw phone numbers, raw calendar URLs, raw chat-room URLs, raw policy bodies, and raw waiver justifications MUST NOT appear; the record carries opaque refs, typed vocabulary, and bounded reviewable summaries only. Adding a new ship-or-no-ship posture, blocker-kind class, mitigation class, export-packet-action class, or stale-evidence summary class is additive-minor and bumps release_truth_summary_schema_version; repurposing an existing value, weakening any allOf invariant, or collapsing two distinct refusal / degraded states into one bare label is breaking and requires a new decision row.

release_truth_summary_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the release-truth summary payload shape. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable identifier safe for live surfaces, support bundles, public-proof packets, claim manifests, release packets, enterprise-review exports, and screenshots. Raw URLs, raw absolute paths, raw email addresses, raw phone numbers, raw chat-room URLs, raw calendar URLs, raw on-call rotation entries, and raw secret material MUST NOT appear here.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short reviewable sentence. Personal handles, raw email addresses, raw phone numbers, raw calendar URLs, raw chat-room URLs, raw on-call rotation entries, raw policy bodies, raw waiver justifications, and raw secret material MUST NOT appear.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional worked-example prelude carried on fixtures. Never part of the runtime record.

release_target_class.description: Closed nine-class release-target vocabulary. Names what kind of release moment the summary grades. Mirrors the publish-target classes on /schemas/release/publish_target.schema.json (Public preview / Stable / LTS / Mirror / Registry / Emergency) plus the governance-bearing publication moments (workflow-bundle certification, claim or archetype publication, public-truth publication, support-window notice, compatibility-window publication, rollback or yank in flight).

ship_or_no_ship_posture_class.description: Closed seven-class ship-or-no-ship posture vocabulary. ship_satisfied_no_blockers = all decision-right cards are satisfied and no blockers, waived blockers, or stale evidence rows are holding ship. ship_with_named_narrowing = ship is approved with at least one named narrowing recorded as a satisfied_with_named_narrowing decision-right card and at least one mitigation entry. hold_blockers_open = at least one open blocker is gating ship; the summary MUST NOT render as 'go' on any consuming surface. hold_waived_blockers_within_expiry = at least one waived blocker is gating ship under an active waiver inside its expiry window. no_ship_blockers_or_unresolved = ship is refused on at least one open blocker or unresolved owner. no_ship_decision_route_unresolved = at least one decision-right card is in blocked_no_decision_route_named; the summary MUST render as degraded with degraded_decision_route_missing rather than collapsing into 'at risk'. rollback_or_yank_in_flight = a previously-published artifact is being rolled back, yanked, revoked, or repinned.

blocker_kind_class.description: Closed twelve-class blocker-kind vocabulary. Names what kind of blocker is gating the release moment. Mirrors the run-state grammar on /schemas/governance/nightly_report_row.schema.json (blocked_by_failure, blocked_by_drift, waiver_expired_failure, evidence_stale) plus the ownership-and-decision-route degradations (ownership_unresolved_failure, decision_route_unresolved_failure).

waived_blocker_authority_class.description: Closed eight-class waived-blocker authority vocabulary. Mirrors /schemas/governance/waiver_expiry_item.schema.json#waiver_authority_class plus the security / compatibility / product-scope councils so the summary, the live tile, and the waiver-expiry queue agree on which body issued the waiver.

expiry_proximity_class.description: Closed six-class expiry-proximity vocabulary. Mirrors /schemas/governance/waiver_expiry_item.schema.json#expiry_proximity_class verbatim so the release-truth summary and the waiver-expiry queue route through the same proximity grammar.

mitigation_class.description: Closed ten-class mitigation vocabulary. Mirrors /schemas/governance/fitness_tile.schema.json#mitigation_note_class for the live-evidence cases and /schemas/governance/nightly_report_row.schema.json#mitigation_status_class for the recurring-evidence cases. Adds escalated_pending_council_decision and rollback_in_flight_pending_revalidation so a release-truth summary can name the meanwhile posture without collapsing into a generic 'in progress' label.

stale_evidence_summary_class.description: Closed four-class stale-evidence summary vocabulary. Pairs with the integer stale_evidence_count so a summary that says 'no stale evidence' cannot also render a non-zero count and a summary with stale evidence cannot collapse into a clean ship posture.

export_packet_action_class.description: Closed nine-class export-packet-action vocabulary. Mirrors the publish-target / break-glass / rollback / yank / revoke / repin actions on /schemas/release/publish_target.schema.json and /schemas/release/rollback_revocation_panel.schema.json so the release-truth summary names the next concrete publication step rather than free-form prose.

degraded_state_label_class.description: Closed eight-class degraded-state-label vocabulary. Mirrors /schemas/governance/decision_right_card.schema.json#degraded_state_label_class plus degraded_rollback_or_yank_in_flight for the rollback-or-yank-in-flight posture. A summary cannot be satisfied and degraded simultaneously.

release_candidate_ref.description: Opaque ref into the underlying release-candidate card on /schemas/release/release_candidate_card.schema.json. Required when release_target_class is in { stable_release_candidate, preview_or_beta_promotion, rollback_or_yank_in_flight }.

publish_target_ref.description: Opaque ref into the publish-target row on /schemas/release/publish_target.schema.json. Required when release_target_class is in { stable_release_candidate, lts_line_creation_or_renewal, preview_or_beta_promotion, rollback_or_yank_in_flight }.

channel_ref.description: Opaque ref into the release channel id on /schemas/release/release_channel_identity.schema.json. Required when release_target_class is in { stable_release_candidate, lts_line_creation_or_renewal, preview_or_beta_promotion, support_window_or_end_of_support_notice, compatibility_window_publication, rollback_or_yank_in_flight }.

lts_line_ref.description: Opaque ref into the LTS-line id. Required to be non-null when release_target_class is lts_line_creation_or_renewal.

headline_label.description: Bounded reviewable headline label rendered onto the summary. Resolves against release_target_class plus the candidate / channel / LTS scope; free-text 'all good' / 'at risk' copy is non-conforming.

release_target_summary.description: One-sentence reviewable summary of the release moment the summary grades. Names the candidate, the channel, and the publish target; raw absolute paths and raw URLs do not appear.

decision_right_card_ref.description: Opaque ref into the decision_right_card_record gating this blocker. Required so the blocker can be reconstructed back to who owns the next decision and why that forum is required.

linked_evidence_ref.description: Opaque ref into the underlying evidence packet, fitness-function row, compatibility row, certified-archetype row, security-egress-class row, claim-manifest row, scorecard lane, or governance packet that captured the blocker. Null only when blocker_kind_class is in { ownership_unresolved_failure, decision_route_unresolved_failure } where the absence of evidence is the blocker.

blocker_summary.description: One-sentence reviewable summary naming what is blocking ship. Raw measurement bytes, raw policy bodies, and raw waiver justifications do not appear.

decision_right_card_ref.description: Opaque ref into the decision_right_card_record (state = satisfied_with_active_waiver) gating this waived blocker. Required so the waiver can be reconstructed back to who held it and which forum approved it.

waiver_ref.description: Opaque ref into the waiver record in artifacts/governance/ownership_matrix.yaml. Required.

waiver_expires_on.description: Expiry timestamp copied from the waiver record. Required to be non-null whenever expiry_proximity_class is anything other than no_expiry_pinned. For expired_past_due the timestamp MUST be in the past relative to evaluated_at.

linked_evidence_ref.description: Opaque ref into the underlying evidence packet, fitness-function row, compatibility row, certified-archetype row, security-egress-class row, claim-manifest row, scorecard lane, or governance packet covered by the waiver.

waiver_summary.description: One-sentence reviewable summary of the waiver posture. Raw waiver justification text MUST NOT appear; the summary names the authority, the expiry, and the held release condition.

linked_evidence_ref.description: Opaque ref into the stale evidence packet, fitness-tile row, nightly-report row, compatibility row, or scorecard lane row holding ship.

decision_right_card_ref.description: Opaque ref into the decision_right_card_record gating this stale-evidence blocker. Null admissible only when the stale evidence is informational (e.g. a near-expiry compatibility row that has not yet aged out).

captured_at.description: captured_at copied from the underlying evidence packet header. Null only when the evidence is missing rather than expired.

expires_at.description: Computed captured_at + stale_after copied from the underlying evidence packet header. Null only when the evidence is missing rather than expired.

stale_evidence_summary.description: One-sentence reviewable summary naming why the evidence is stale (time window passed, rerun trigger fired, missing) and what is held meanwhile.

what_users_should_do.description: One-sentence reviewable instruction for the user / consumer. Resolves against mitigation_class; free-text fear copy is non-conforming.

what_operators_should_do.description: One-sentence reviewable instruction for the lane DRI / operator. Resolves against mitigation_class.

mitigation_summary.description: One-sentence reviewable summary describing what is narrower, slower, less portable, temporarily blocked, waiver-held, partial-profile, escalated, or rolling back. Surfaces MUST NOT collapse this into a generic 'see release notes' link.

release_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref into the release-evidence packet that the action publishes, holds, or rolls back. Required when export_packet_action_class is in { publish_to_target_after_concurrence, hold_publish_pending_blocker, hold_publish_pending_waiver_renewal, escalate_for_break_glass_review }.

publish_target_ref.description: Opaque ref into the publish-target row on /schemas/release/publish_target.schema.json the action would publish to. Required when export_packet_action_class is in { publish_to_target_after_concurrence, hold_publish_pending_blocker, hold_publish_pending_waiver_renewal, mirror_only_emergency_push, escalate_for_break_glass_review }.

rollback_or_revocation_panel_ref.description: Opaque ref into the rollback-or-revocation panel on /schemas/release/rollback_revocation_panel.schema.json. Required when export_packet_action_class is in { rollback_to_prior_stable, yank_or_revoke_active_artifact, repin_supported_floor }.

support_export_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref into the support-export packet that downstream support / enterprise reviews consume. Required when render_on_support_export = true on the parent record.

action_summary.description: One-sentence reviewable summary naming the next concrete publication step. Free-text 'see release notes' copy is non-conforming.

export_fields.description: Visual / export parity floor. Every consuming surface that renders the summary MUST render the same release_target, ship_or_no_ship_posture_class, blockers, waived_blockers, stale_evidence_count, mitigations, export_packet_action, linked_decision_right_card_refs, and degraded_state_label_class. Setting render_on_release_packet, render_on_support_export, render_on_governance_packet, render_on_claim_manifest, or render_on_enterprise_review to true signals that the summary is part of public / release / support truth and MUST satisfy the per-state export rules in the schema's allOf block.

release_truth_summary_id.description: Stable, machine-readable id quoted by the dashboard, the milestone scorecard, the release-evidence shiproom packet, the support bundle, the public claim manifest, the compatibility-window report, and the enterprise / managed-tenant review.

evaluated_at.description: Timestamp at which the summary was projected. Distinct from the underlying evidence captured_at and decision-right card evaluated_at; the summary can be reprojected when a blocker closes, a waiver expires, or stale evidence ages out.

headline_label.description: Bounded reviewable headline label rendered onto the summary. Resolves against ship_or_no_ship_posture_class plus release_target_class; free-text 'all good' / 'all bad' copy is non-conforming.

blockers.description: Open blockers gating this release moment. Empty admissible only when ship_or_no_ship_posture_class is in { ship_satisfied_no_blockers, ship_with_named_narrowing, hold_waived_blockers_within_expiry, rollback_or_yank_in_flight }.

waived_blockers.description: Waived blockers gating this release moment under an active waiver inside its expiry window. Required to be non-empty when ship_or_no_ship_posture_class = hold_waived_blockers_within_expiry.

stale_evidence_count.description: Count of stale evidence rows holding ship. Pairs with stale_evidence_summary_class and stale_evidence_entries; zero when stale_evidence_summary_class = no_stale_evidence.

stale_evidence_entries.description: Typed stale-evidence entries. Length MUST match stale_evidence_count up to the cap; surfaces MAY render the cap label when stale_evidence_summary_class = many_stale_evidence_rows_capped.

mitigations.description: Typed mitigations naming what is narrower, slower, less portable, temporarily blocked, waiver-held, partial-profile, escalated, or rolling back. At least one entry is required when ship_or_no_ship_posture_class is in { ship_with_named_narrowing, hold_blockers_open, hold_waived_blockers_within_expiry, no_ship_blockers_or_unresolved, no_ship_decision_route_unresolved, rollback_or_yank_in_flight }.

linked_decision_right_card_refs.description: Refs into the decision_right_card_records that gate this summary. Required to be non-empty so the ship-or-no-ship posture is always reconstructable back to who can decide the next move and why the forum is required. Each blocker_entry, waived_blocker_entry, and stale_evidence_entry decision_right_card_ref MUST appear in this array.

degraded_state_summary.description: One-sentence reviewable summary of the degraded-state label. Names the unresolved owner, the missing decision route, the failed concurrence, the stale evidence, the expired waiver, the past-deadline no-decision case, or the rollback / yank in flight; raw policy bodies and raw waiver justifications do not appear.

allOf.description: ship_satisfied_no_blockers requires no blockers, no waived blockers, zero stale evidence, no mitigations other than no_mitigation_required_satisfied, an export action of publish or no-action, and the not-degraded label.
ship_with_named_narrowing requires at least one mitigation entry naming the narrowing and forces the not-degraded label.
hold_blockers_open requires at least one blocker entry, at least one mitigation entry, and an export action of hold_publish_pending_blocker.
hold_waived_blockers_within_expiry requires at least one waived blocker, no expired-past-due waivers, and the waiver-holds-release-until-expiry mitigation.
no_ship_blockers_or_unresolved requires at least one blocker entry and an export action of hold_publish_pending_blocker, rollback, yank-or-revoke, or escalate-for-break-glass.

no_ship_decision_route_unresolved requires at least one blocker of kind decision_route_unresolved_failure or ownership_unresolved_failure and forces the degraded_decision_route_missing label so 'at risk' without a named forum cannot appear complete.
rollback_or_yank_in_flight requires the rollback_or_yank_in_flight mitigation, a non-null rollback_or_revocation_panel_ref, and the degraded_rollback_or_yank_in_flight label.
stale_evidence_count = 0 forces stale_evidence_summary_class = no_stale_evidence and stale_evidence_entries empty.
stale_evidence_count >= 1 forces stale_evidence_summary_class out of no_stale_evidence and stale_evidence_entries to be non-empty so a non-zero count cannot collapse into a clean ship posture.
publish_to_target_after_concurrence requires a non-null release_packet_ref and a non-null publish_target_ref.
rollback_to_prior_stable, yank_or_revoke_active_artifact, and repin_supported_floor require a non-null rollback_or_revocation_panel_ref.

Each waived_blocker entry whose expiry_proximity_class = expired_past_due forces the parent posture out of hold_waived_blockers_within_expiry and into no_ship_blockers_or_unresolved so a lapsed waiver cannot render as held.
